summary: Large gmregclear decompilation PR for the game-mode regular clear/adventure/multiman area. It replaced many nonmatching stubs in src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c with C implementations, expanded local structs and BSS layout knowledge, and upgraded gmregclear.h/types.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_T toward typed signatures. The only human note says regressions were caused by data locality and expected to be solved when linking, so review intent is weak beyond that.
what_changed: Implemented a large block of previously stubbed gmregclear.c functions, including gm_8017CE34, fn_8017D9C0, gm_8017DB88, fn_8017DD7C, fn_8017E8A4, fn_8017EE40, fn_8017F1B8, fn_8017F2A4, fn_8017F47C, fn_8017F608, fn_8017FA1C, fn_8017FBA4, fn_8017FE54, fn_8017FF1C, fn_801803FC, fn_80180630, fn_80180C60, fn_80181598, fn_80181708, fn_80181C80, fn_80181E18, gm_80182174, gm_80182578, fn_80182B5C, and gm_80182DF0.

Expanded gmregclear.c include dependencies for baselib objects/controllers/text, game-mode helpers, interface HUD helpers, language/card/time libraries, item and fighter libraries, and stage/ground helpers needed by the new C implementations.

Refined local data layouts: lbl_80472D28_t was expanded with HSD_JObj pointers, HSD_ImageDesc, HSD_Archive, DynamicModelDesc, HSD_Text, scalar fields, and explicit byte fields; lbl_80472E48_t grew from size 0x80 to 0x90 with a new STATIC_ASSERT.

Added local typed table/entry structs RegClearSpawnEntry and RegClearCharEntry and replaced an opaque lbl_80472ED8 byte blob with a structured BSS layout containing PlayerInitData, spawn-entry arrays/pointers, and fields at offsets x6BC through x6CD.
Added data table lbl_803D8B88 as a u16 array with ten stage/character-like constants in gmregclear.c.

Updated gmregclear.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete return/argument types for many functions, including typed StartMeleeData, UnkAdventureData, HSD_Text, HSD_GObj, PlayerInitData, InternalStageId, and function pointer callback signatures.

Updated types.h so Unk1PData.xC.x24 entries use f32 fields for x4/x8 instead of int, added a u16 xE in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t layout, and typed several UnkAdventureData callback pointers such as x48, x50, x58, x64, and x68.

Implemented gm_8017CE34 as a substantial StartMeleeData setup routine that configures rules, player slots, enemy colors, Adventure/regular-clear flags, stage-derived values, callback-driven CPU/color/data fields, and special cases such as sentinel character 0x21 and c_kind values.

Implemented fn_8017D9C0 as a character-selection helper that shuffles lbl_803D79F0 with HSD_Randi, skips characters already in supplied exclusion arrays, checks gm_80164840, and falls back to sentinel 0x21.
Implemented gm_80182DF0 to populate a static UnkMultimanData result from lbl_803D8D08 with case-specific offsets for multiman modes 33 through 38.
smart_moves: Used explicit offset-annotated struct fields and STATIC_ASSERTs to lock down inferred layout while still keeping unknown names as x-offset fields.
Converted header prototypes as matching evidence became available instead of leaving broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations, improving cross-file type checking for future work.
Modeled callback fields in UnkAdventureData with concrete signatures, which makes gm_8017CE34/gm_8017DB88 call sites more readable and prevents argument-width drift.
Kept address-based function names such as fn_8017D9C0 and gm_8017CE34 where semantic names were not yet proven, avoiding overconfident naming.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK calls, explicit casts between u8/s8/s32, function pointer casts, and pointer arithmetic for StartMeleeData player slots.
Structured lbl_80472ED8 around real offsets instead of a monolithic pad, enabling later decompilation to reuse spawn-entry pointers and PlayerInitData fields.
decomp_lessons: When a gm file has many interconnected stubs, matching one large setup function can require typing callbacks, player init structs, BSS globals, and header declarations together.
For mode data tables, sentinel values such as 0x21 should be preserved explicitly until their semantic enum name is confirmed.
Large BSS globals can be progressively recovered by replacing pad blobs with offset-accurate fields and retaining padding where meaning is unknown.
Function pointer callback types in shared structs are high-value: once recovered, they improve both caller implementations and future table-driven mode code.
Data locality can cause regressions even when source logic is closer to matched; the PR comment explicitly notes regressions were due to data locality and expected to be resolved when linking.
Use STATIC_ASSERT after resizing inferred structs, especially when a struct size changes, as lbl_80472E48_t did from 0x80 to 0x90.
Keep local temporary variable types and signedness close to the assembly, especially in branch-heavy setup code using u8, s8, and s32 comparisons.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-based names with gm_ or fn_ prefixes, e.g. gm_8017CE34, fn_8017D9C0, fn_80180C60.
New partially understood structs use descriptive module prefixes when possible, e.g. RegClearSpawnEntry and RegClearCharEntry.
Unknown fields are named by offset, such as x6BC, x6BE, x6C0, x6CD, x48, x64, and x68.
Padding fields are named pad_<offset>, preserving layout without inventing semantics.
Global labels remain lbl_80xxxxxx when no semantic name is proven, e.g. lbl_80472ED8, lbl_803D8B88, lbl_803D8D08.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(24) and PAD_STACK(8) in implemented functions where needed for stack matching.
Used explicit u8/s8/s32 casts around comparisons and callback arguments to match PowerPC integer promotion behavior.
Preserved pointer arithmetic into StartMeleeData player records, including increments of 0x24 and base offset 0x60, rather than forcing a cleaner abstraction that might not match.
Represented table slicing with byte-pointer casts and fixed offsets in gm_80182DF0, e.g. base + 0x6C, 0x88, 0x12C, 0x148, and other case-specific offsets.
Used function pointer casts for rule callbacks where exact typedefs were not fully established.
Kept sentinel-driven loops and fallbacks using 0x21 in character arrays instead of replacing them with speculative constants.
Used HSD_Randi shuffling in fn_8017D9C0 to mirror random selection behavior while keeping the original data buffer lbl_803D79F0 as the base.

review_feedback.text: Regressions are caused by data locality, will be solved when linking
review_feedback.implication: Any observed regressions were acknowledged as link/data-placement related rather than necessarily source-logic errors.

review_feedback.text: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.implication: Reviewer-specific rationale and requested changes cannot be inferred from this dump.

follow_up_queries: Check later gmregclear PRs for semantic renames of RegClearSpawnEntry, RegClearCharEntry, lbl_80472ED8, and the 0x21 sentinel.
Search for PRs that resolved the data-locality regressions mentioned in the issue comment.
Inspect later UnkAdventureData typing to see whether x5C/x60 callbacks were eventually given concrete signatures.
Compare subsequent gmregclear.h changes for functions still left as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS after this PR, such as fn_80181E18, gm_80182174, fn_80182B5C in the excerpt.
